perturbation.original_text: Client's Competitors means those merchants identified in Exhibit D attached hereto. Client may update 2 CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS OMITTED AND FILED SEPARATELY WITH THE SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION. ASTERISKS DENOTE OMISSIONS. this list in writing not more than once every six (6) months by adding other merchants whose primary business is reselling Vitamins upon the mutual agreement of Excite.
perturbation.changed_text: Client's Competitors means initially those merchants identified in Exhibit D attached hereto. Client may unilaterally update 2 CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS OMITTED AND FILED SEPARATELY WITH THE SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION. ASTERISKS DENOTE OMISSIONS. this list in writing at any time by adding other merchants whose business involves selling or promoting health-related products, without requiring any agreement from Excite.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires 'mutual agreement of Excite' for adding competitors to Exhibit D. The modified text allows Client to unilaterally update this list at any time without Excite's agreement, including entities that aren't direct vitamin resellers. This could lead to disputes over which entities Excite is restricted from promoting. This change causes an in-text contradiction with later sections where 'Client's Competitors' affects Excite's advertising obligations and exclusivity promises.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Client will be promoted as the preferred and dominant reseller of Vitamins in the Health Channel on the WebCrawler Site during the term of this Agreement. As such, Excite may not display banner advertising and/or promotional placements for any of Client's Competitors that are, in the aggregate, of equal or greater prominence and exposure than the aggregate of Client's links, advertising banners and promotional placements on the pages of the Health Channel on the WebCrawler Site during the term of this Agreement. For purposes of this Agreement, Client's Competitors means those merchants identified in Exhibit D attached hereto. Client may update 2 CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS OMITTED AND FILED SEPARATELY WITH THE SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION. ASTERISKS DENOTE OMISSIONS. this list in writing not more than once every six (6) months by adding other merchants whose primary business is reselling Vitamins upon the mutual agreement of Excite.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text regarding the process for updating 'Client's Competitors' in Exhibit D. The original and contradicted text require 'mutual agreement of Excite' for adding competitors, while the changed text allows the Client to unilaterally update the list without Excite's agreement. This creates a clear conflict and would lead to uncertainty about the enforceability of the exclusivity clauses related to Client's Competitors. The change directly alters a core obligation related to who is considered a 'Client Competitor'.

perturbation.original_text: Excite will have sole control over the "look and feel" of the Excite Network including, but not limited to, the display, appearance and placement of the parties' respective names and/or brands and the promotional links, but such control shall not permit Excite to modify Client's logos and trademarks and it does not relieve Excite from its obligations regarding Client's preferred and dominant sponsorship status as set forth elsewhere in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Excite will have sole control over the "look and feel" of the Excite Network including, but not limited to, the display, appearance and placement of the parties' respective names and/or brands and the promotional links. Excite reserves the right to alter the Client's logos and trademarks to fit the overall aesthetic of the Excite Network, and such control supersedes any obligations regarding Client's preferred and dominant sponsorship status as set forth elsewhere in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly prevents Excite from modifying Client's logos and trademarks and preserves Client's sponsorship status. The modified text grants Excite the right to alter logos and trademarks and subordinates Client's sponsorship status to Excite's aesthetic control. This creates a direct in-text contradiction regarding trademark use and sponsorship guarantees. This change contradicts earlier promises that assure the client of preferred and dominant reseller status.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Client will be promoted as the preferred and dominant reseller of Vitamins in the Health Channel on the WebCrawler Site during the term of this Agreement. As such, Excite may not display banner advertising and/or promotional placements for any of Client's Competitors that are, in the aggregate, of equal or greater prominence and exposure than the aggregate of Client's links, advertising banners and promotional placements on the pages of the Health Channel on the WebCrawler Site during the term of this Agreement. For purposes of this Agreement, Client's Competitors means those merchants identified in Exhibit D attached hereto. Client may update 2 CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS OMITTED AND FILED SEPARATELY WITH THE SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION. ASTERISKS DENOTE OMISSIONS. this list in writing not more than once every six (6) months by adding other merchants whose primary business is reselling Vitamins upon the mutual agreement of Excite.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants Excite the right to alter the Client's logos and trademarks, and subordinates the Client's preferred and dominant sponsorship status. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that the Client will be promoted as the preferred and dominant reseller and that Excite may not display banner advertising for any of Client's Competitors that are of equal or greater prominence. The change undermines the Client's assured dominant reseller status by allowing Excite to modify logos and prioritize other sponsorships, creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Excite guarantees to deliver the annual impressions totals set forth in Exhibit C hereto. If Excite fails to deliver the indicated number of impressions required during any annual period, Client may suspend (but not eliminate) its payments specified in Section 7 for a maximum of sixty (60) days (the "Make-Good Period) during which Excite will deliver the shortfall of such impressions.
perturbation.changed_text: Excite guarantees to make a reasonable effort to deliver the annual impressions totals set forth in Exhibit C hereto. If Excite fails to deliver the indicated number of impressions required during any annual period, Client’s sole remedy is to request in good faith that Excite extend the term of the agreement by up to thirty (30) days, during which Excite will attempt to deliver the shortfall of such impressions. Suspension of payments is not permitted.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides a clear 'guarantee' of impressions and allows payment suspension as a remedy. The modified text replaces 'guarantee' with 'reasonable effort,' eliminates payment suspension, and offers only a term extension for shortfall delivery. This creates an in-text contradiction because the original section implied a strong commitment and financial recourse for the Client, while the modification significantly weakens Excite's obligations and the Client's options if Excite fails to deliver. This weakens the value proposition of this agreement for the client.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Client will pay Excite sponsorship and advertising fees of [*****] for the first twelve (12) month period following the Launch Date ([*****]). These fees will be paid in twelve (12) equal monthly installments of [*****]. The first monthly payment for [*****] will be due one month following the Launch Date. Subsequent installments will be due on a monthly basis thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The original text guarantees impression delivery with payment suspension as a remedy for failure. The changed text weakens this guarantee to a 'reasonable effort,' eliminates payment suspension, and replaces it with a term extension. The contradicted text specifies fixed payment installments for a defined period. The change directly contradicts this as the guaranteed impressions are no longer secured, payment suspension is disallowed despite failure to deliver, and the remedy of term extension affects the payment schedule, creating significant financial uncertainty for the client. This alters the payment terms and guaranteed services, causing an in-text contradiction.
